issuesFound.title: missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty. A descriptive page title is required for users to identify the purpose of the page and navigate between open tabs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible Design & Productivity Tools</title>

issuesFound.title: toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: This button toggles the main navigation menu but lacks an aria-expanded attribute. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 38: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: broken ARIA reference: element references id that does not exist
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-controls attribute references "resources-submenu", but the target <ul> on line 71 has no id attribute (or the id is missing from the inventory).
issuesFound.fix: Line 71: <ul id="resources-submenu" role="list" hidden>

issuesFound.title: non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" is non-descriptive when taken out of context. Users navigating by links alone will not know what they are reading more about.
issuesFound.fix: Line 134: Read more about our features (no credit card required)

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule sets outline to none. While it sets an outline-offset, it provides no visible replacement (like a box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline), leaving keyboard users with no indication of which element is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 314: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the focus indicator for browsers or states not matching :focus-visible. If :focus-visible is not supported or fails to trigger, the user has no visual focus feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on @media (forced-colors: active) :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: In high-contrast mode, the focus indicator is explicitly removed. Users with low vision relying on system-forced colors will have no way to track their focus position.
issuesFound.fix: Line 326: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component explicitly removes the outline on focus-visible but does not provide a box-shadow or border replacement within this rule block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 514: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the logo is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 614: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus indicator. Keyboard users on mobile-width screens will not know when they have reached the menu trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 648: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links remove the outline on focus. Although an outline-offset is provided, the outline width is zero (none), resulting in no visual indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 711: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the default outline. While it adds a border-color and box-shadow, the box-shadow uses a low-opacity blue (0.2) which may not be visible enough to serve as a primary focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 748: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the focus indicator for the search input when using focus-visible, providing no alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 755: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus indicator without replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 778: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 799: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the focus indicator without replacement.

issuesFound.explanation: The pricing billing toggle buttons remove the focus indicator without replacement.

issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table container is focusable (tabindex="0") but removes its focus indicator, leaving keyboard users unaware they are focused on the region.

issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion triggers remove the focus indicator without replacement.

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum of 24px for interactive targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 581: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons are explicitly sized to 20px by 20px, making them difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or those using touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 595: min-width: 24px; Line 596: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle is sized at 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. While it meets the 24px minimum, it lacks sufficient spacing to reach 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 635: min-height: 44px; Line 636: min-width: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have a min-height of 28px. This is significantly smaller than the 44px recommended size for primary navigation elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 683: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links have a min-height of 28px, making them difficult to target on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 706: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is set to 28px, which is below the recommended 44px for form inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 731: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28px by 28px, which is below the recommended 44px target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 761: width: 44px; Line 762: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Filter buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1136: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1286: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .product-body .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: Product card buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1234: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class resets width/height to auto and overflow to visible. This breaks the standard visually-hidden pattern and may cause the content to become visible or fail to be properly clipped, potentially interfering with layout or AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 343: width: 1px; Line 344: height: 1px; Line 347: overflow: hidden; Line 348: clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0);

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition for its appearance. There is no override in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable this movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 412: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes a transform transition. There is no reduced-motion override to disable the "active" state movement or hover transitions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 412: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The "→" icon in feature links moves on hover via transform. While there is a partial override on line 1014, it only targets the hover state, not the base transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1014: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a::after { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion icon rotates via transform. While there is an override on line 1569, it only disables the transition, not the movement itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1569: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body text is set to word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word spacing pushes words too close together, significantly impairing readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 214: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body text is set to letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative letter spacing reduces the legibility of characters, especially at smaller font sizes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 215: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: The secondary text color (--colour-text-secondary) and focus ring color (--colour-focus-ring) are set to #aaaaaa. Against a white background (#ffffff), this results in a contrast ratio of 2.3:1, which is below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 68: --colour-text-secondary: #666666; Line 101: --colour-focus-ring: #666666;

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #ee9999 / background #ffffff ratio 2.4:1
issuesFound.explanation: The error color (--colour-error) used for required markers is #ee9999. Against a white background, this fails the 4.5:1 contrast ratio required for informative text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 58: --colour-error: #d93030;

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global 'a' selector removes text-decoration. In body text (like paragraphs), links will only be distinguishable by color, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with color blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 238: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation drawer toggle the visibility of the menu but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `_open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1561: `_open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle switch for month/year billing updates a visual class on the track but does not update the `aria-pressed` or `aria-checked` state on the toggle element itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2371: `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear); setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, isYear);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the inline validator adds a visual error class and updates an error message but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3145: `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is validated as successful, the `_renderResult` function removes the error class but does not set `aria-invalid` to `false` or remove the attribute. The field may continue to be announced as invalid by some screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3140: `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not initialise the `aria-expanded` state to `false`. This can lead to an undefined state for screen readers until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader` controller hides and shows the header based on scroll direction using CSS classes but does not update `aria-hidden`. This allows keyboard users to potentially tab into links within a visually hidden header.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1885: `addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` Line 1890: `removeClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the view mode is toggled between grid and list in the product filter, the change is visual only. There is no announcement to screen reader users that the layout of the product results has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2165: `_productFilterState.viewMode = mode; liveRegion.announce(`Layout changed to ${mode} view`);`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form email input is a personal data field but lacks the `autocomplete` attribute. This fails WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3365: `<input type="email" autocomplete="email" ...>` (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` binds a `click` listener to a `div` (the zoneEl) but does not provide a `keydown` handler for the `Space` key, only `Enter`. Non-button interactive elements must support both keys.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3885: `if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); }`

issuesFound.title: Programmatic focus not managed on dialog close
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent` banner is a modal dialog. When the user accepts or declines, the banner is removed from the DOM, but focus is not explicitly returned to the element that triggered it (or a logical starting point like the skip link).
issuesFound.fix: Line 4185: `_hideBanner(); if (_triggerEl) _triggerEl.focus();` (where _triggerEl is the stored activeElement from before the banner opened)

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle switch for month/year billing updates a visual class on the track but does not update the `aria-pressed` or `aria-checked` state on the toggle element itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2371: `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear); setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, isYear);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the inline validator adds a visual error class and updates an error message but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3145: `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is validated as successful, the `_renderResult` function removes the error class but does not set `aria-invalid` to `false` or remove the attribute. The field may continue to be announced as invalid by some screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3140: `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not initialise the `aria-expanded` state to `false`. This can lead to an undefined state for screen readers until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader` controller hides and shows the header based on scroll direction using CSS classes but does not update `aria-hidden`. This allows keyboard users to potentially tab into links within a visually hidden header.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1885: `addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` Line 1890: `removeClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the view mode is toggled between grid and list in the product filter, the change is visual only. There is no announcement to screen reader users that the layout of the product results has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2165: `_productFilterState.viewMode = mode; liveRegion.announce(`Layout changed to ${mode} view`);`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form email input is a personal data field but lacks the `autocomplete` attribute. This fails WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3365: `<input type="email" autocomplete="email" ...>` (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` binds a `click` listener to a `div` (the zoneEl) but does not provide a `keydown` handler for the `Space` key, only `Enter`. Non-button interactive elements must support both keys.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3885: `if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); }`

issuesFound.title: Programmatic focus not managed on dialog close
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent` banner is a modal dialog. When the user accepts or declines, the banner is removed from the DOM, but focus is not explicitly returned to the element that triggered it (or a logical starting point like the skip link).
issuesFound.fix: Line 4185: `_hideBanner(); if (_triggerEl) _triggerEl.focus();` (where _triggerEl is the stored activeElement from before the banner opened)

fpTitles: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
Programmatic focus not managed on dialog close

issuesFound.title: decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron indicator inside the navigation dropdown trigger is purely decorative as the button already has a text label, but it is not hidden from screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 895: <span className="nav-link__chevron" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input has a validation state managed by the `email` hook, but it does not use the aria-invalid attribute to programmatically communicate when the field is in an error state.

'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus. People who rely on keyboard navigation are severely impacted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 317: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule removes the focus outline for mouse-click focus states without ensuring a visible alternative exists for keyboard focus. This can completely hide focus for keyboard users if the :focus-visible style is also missing or invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The focus outline is removed for the logo link without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when this interactive element is focused, breaking navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 445: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button loses its focus outline without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on this critical interactive control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 473: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility sets position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which does not hide content visually or from screen readers. This breaks screen-reader-only text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 273: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform transitions without a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unnecessary movement when interacting with buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 367: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has transform and box-shadow transitions without a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unnecessary movement on hover.
issuesFound.fix: Line 660: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); } }

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses word spacing and can reduce readability, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2154: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 2160: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions change the visibility of dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the expanded state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2248: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2260: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle button (switch between monthly/annual billing) does not set aria-pressed to reflect its state. Screen reader users will not know the current toggle state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3175: in _syncToggleState, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` inside the forEach loop. Line 3180: in _onToggleClick, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, next === 'year');` after `setPeriod(next);`.

issuesFound.title: product filter update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the visible product grid but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users may not know that the product list has been filtered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2680: after `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied.');`.

issuesFound.title: form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function in srUtils adds an error description but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers may not announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3758: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` as the first line inside the function.

issuesFound.title: dropdown triggers not initialised with aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are not initialised with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2318: in the register function, after setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobile nav trigger not initialised with aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded on page load. Screen readers may not know the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2132: in mobileNav.init, after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: dynamically hidden mobile nav drawer not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is hidden visually but not removed from the accessibility tree (aria-hidden not set). Screen reader users may still navigate into the hidden drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2154: in open(), add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`. Line 2160: in close(), add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: user action (pricing period change) not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user changes the billing period, the prices update but there is no live region announcement to inform screen reader users of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3160: in setPeriod, after `_pricingState.period = period;`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: form validation error clearing does not reset aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error description but does not clear aria-invalid on the field. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3765: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` as the first line inside the function.

issuesFound.title: non-native interactive element (pricing toggle) missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle element (_toggleEl) has a click listener but no keydown handler for Enter and Space, making it inaccessible to keyboard users who cannot activate it with the keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3175: add `_toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onToggleClick(); } });` after the click event listener.

issuesFound.title: dynamically hidden dropdown panels not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown panels are closed, they are hidden visually but not removed from the accessibility tree (aria-hidden not set). Screen reader users may still navigate into hidden panels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2248: in _openInstance, add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line 2260: in _closeInstance, add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` before `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button is missing aria-controls pointing to the panel id, so screen reader users may not know which panel is controlled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2318: in register, ensure `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, panelId);` is called (it is present, but if missing, add it).

issuesFound.title: form validation error not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the error message is displayed but not announced via a live region. Screen reader users may not hear the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3758: in markInvalid, after adding describedby, add `liveRegion.announceAssertive(errorEl.textContent);`.

issuesFound.title: user action (search filter) not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user applies a search filter, the results update but there is no live region announcement to inform screen reader users of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2680: after the emit call, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.searchResultsFound ? STRINGS.announce.searchResultsFound(totalMatches, s.searchQuery) : 'Search results updated.');`.

issuesFound.title: dynamically hidden FAQ answers not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ answers are collapsed, they are hidden visually but not removed from the accessibility tree (aria-hidden not set). Screen reader users may still navigate into hidden answers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3448: in open, add `setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);` after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line 3458: in close, add `setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` before `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: auto-play carousel without pause control for reduced motion
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonials carousel auto-plays without a user-controllable pause/stop mechanism for users who prefer reduced motion, violating SC 2.2.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3530: in init, ensure the play/pause button is always present and functional, and add `if (prefersReducedMotion()) pause();` after the auto-play check.

issuesFound.title: non-native interactive element (scroll-to-top button) missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button has a click listener but no keydown handler for Enter and Space, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2400: add `_btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onClick(); } });` after the click event listener.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have its aria-expanded attribute updated when the menu is opened or closed. Screen reader users are not informed of the menu state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In mobileNav.close, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: product filter updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the updated product count and grid are not announced to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the content has changed, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters, after updating the grid, add: liveRegion.announce(`Now showing ${totalMatches} products.`);

issuesFound.title: form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are invalid, the aria-invalid attribute is not set, so screen readers are not alerted to the error state. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: In srUtils.markInvalid, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers not initialised with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers are not initialised with aria-expanded="false" on page load, so screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state. This violates WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register, add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden with the hidden attribute but not with aria-hidden, which may not be sufficient for some screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close, add: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); In mobileNav.open, add: _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is changed (monthly/annual), there is no announcement to screen readers, so users may not know the prices have updated. This violates WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.setPeriod, add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${period} billing.`);

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles the dropdown menu does not have aria-expanded attribute, preventing screen reader users from knowing if the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: In NavDropdown component, add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: <button ... aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} ...>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button does not have aria-expanded, hiding the expand/collapse state from screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: <button ... aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} ...>

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile nav toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles the mobile navigation menu does not have aria-expanded, so screen reader users cannot know if the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: <button ... aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} ...>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative chevron icon is not hidden from assistive technology, causing redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span: ...

issuesFound.title: MobileNav chevron icons missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Decorative chevron icons are read by screen readers, adding noise.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to each span: {isExpanded ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent chevron icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon is decorative but not hidden from AT, leading to unnecessary announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span: ...

issuesFound.title: StarRating star icons missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual star icons are not hidden from screen readers, while the rating is already announced via aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to each star span: ...

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button for theme switching has no accessible name, so screen reader users cannot understand its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label to the IconButton: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} ...>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in the mobile nav has no accessible name, making it unusable for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Close navigation menu" to the IconButton: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" ...>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel prev IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The previous button in the carousel is icon-only without an accessible name, so its function is not announced to AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the IconButton: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" ...>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The next button in the carousel lacks an accessible name, hiding its purpose from screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" to the IconButton: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" ...>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The text input does not convey invalid state to assistive technology when invalid prop is true, hindering error perception for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-invalid={invalid} to the input: <input ... aria-invalid={invalid} ...>

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea does not announce invalid state to AT users when invalid prop is true.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-invalid={invalid} to the textarea: <textarea ... aria-invalid={invalid} ...>

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The select element does not communicate invalid state to screen readers when invalid prop is true.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-invalid={invalid} to the select: <select ... aria-invalid={invalid} ...>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons do not have aria-pressed to indicate active state, so screen reader users cannot know which filter is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to each button: <button ... aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} ...>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons lack aria-pressed, hiding the selected state from assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} and aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} to the respective buttons: <button ... aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} ...> and <button ... aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} ...>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in navigation does not have aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot easily identify the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current="page" to the anchor when item.current is true: <a ... aria-current={item.current ? "page" : undefined} ...>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in mobile navigation lacks aria-current, preventing AT users from knowing the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current="page" to the anchor when item.current is true: <a ... aria-current={item.current ? "page" : undefined} ...>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section landmark does not have an accessible name, making it difficult for screen reader users to navigate.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" ...>

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section landmark is not labelled, hindering navigation for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading" to the section: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" ...>

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card article does not have an accessible name, so screen reader users cannot understand its content context.
issuesFound.fix: Add an id to the heading and aria-labelledby to the article: First, add id={headingId} to the h3: <h3 id={headingId} ...>, then add aria-labelledby={headingId} to the article: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} ...>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker for required fields is read as "star" by screen readers, which is confusing since the required state is already announced via VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span: *

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The button does not announce its busy state to assistive technology when loading, so users might not know it's processing.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-busy={isLoading} to the button: <button ... aria-busy={isLoading} ...>

issuesFound.title: Button spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual spinner is not hidden from screen readers, causing redundant announcements since the loading state is already communicated via VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the spinner span: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badges group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The group of trust badges has no accessible name, so screen reader users might not understand its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Trust badges" to the div: <div className="hero-section__trust" aria-label="Trust badges">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop is a decorative overlay but not hidden from assistive technology, causing unnecessary navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the div: <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria-hidden="true" ...>

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay is not content and should be hidden from screen readers to avoid confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the div: <div className="modal-overlay" aria-hidden="true" ...>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list for visual effect is not hidden from assistive technology, leading to duplicate announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the second ul: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

fpTitles: ThemeToggle IconButton missing aria-label
StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
HeroSection trust badges group missing aria-label
MobileNav backdrop missing aria-hidden
Modal overlay missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown component's trigger button controls a submenu but does not have aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to screen reader users. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1015: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation dropdown trigger button controls a submenu but lacks aria-expanded to indicate its state. Screen reader users cannot know if the submenu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1163: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button controls an answer panel but does not have aria-expanded. This prevents screen reader users from knowing if the answer is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1869: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon (▲/▼) inside the NavDropdown trigger is decorative and duplicates the state communicated by aria-expanded. It should be hidden from assistive technology to avoid redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1017: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dropdown chevron icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon (▲/▼) inside the mobile navigation dropdown trigger is purely decorative and should be hidden from screen readers to prevent duplicate information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1165: {isExpanded ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause icon (▶/⏸) inside the video toggle button is decorative because the button has an aria-label. The icon should be hidden from assistive technology to avoid duplicate announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1345: {isPlaying ? '⏸' : '▶'}

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The star symbols (★/☆) inside the StarRating component are decorative visual indicators. The rating is already communicated via aria-label on the parent span. The stars should be hidden from screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 792: {stars.map((filled, i) => ({filled ? '★' : '☆'}))}

issuesFound.title: ComparisonCell tick/dash symbols missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The tick (✓) and dash (—) symbols in the comparison table cells are decorative because their meaning is conveyed via aria-label on the <td>. The symbols should be hidden from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1559: ✓ Fix: Line 1562: —

issuesFound.title: PlanCard feature checkmark icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The checkmark icon (✓) next to each plan feature is decorative and should be hidden from screen readers to avoid repetitive and meaningless announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1645: ✓ {feature}

issuesFound.title: SiteFooter social link icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The social media icon inside the footer link is decorative because the link has an aria-label. The icon should be hidden from assistive technology to prevent duplicate information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2025: <span aria-hidden="true" className="social-icon" />

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without providing the required aria-label prop. Icon-only buttons must have an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} aria-label={LABELS[theme]} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel prev/next IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next carousel buttons are IconButton components without aria-label props. Icon-only buttons must have accessible names to indicate their purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1795: <IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} aria-label="Previous testimonial" className="testimonials-track__prev">
  Fix: Line 1799: <IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} aria-label="Next testimonial" className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons show active state visually but lack aria-pressed to communicate the selection to screen reader users. This violates WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1605: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>
  Fix: Line 1611: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection is a major page region implemented as a <section> but has no aria-label or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify this region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1305: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section has a visually hidden heading but the <section> element is not labelled by it. This prevents screen reader users from understanding the purpose of this landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label. When multiple navigation regions exist, each must have a unique accessible name for screen reader navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 775: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label. This primary navigation region needs an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1035: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard component renders an <article> element without aria-label or aria-labelledby. Each pricing plan card should have an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1625: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required asterisk missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk (*) is read as "star" by screen readers, which is confusing. It should be hidden since the required state is already communicated via the VisuallyHidden "(required)" text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 885: *

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component does not set aria-busy when isLoading is true. Screen reader users should be informed that the button is in a busy state while processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 840: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner inside the Button component is visible but should be hidden from assistive technology because the loading state is announced via the VisuallyHidden loadingLabel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 847: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the desktop navigation has a visual indicator but does not have aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify the current page link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1055: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile navigation has a visual indicator but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users need this to identify the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1180: <a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified whether the menu is currently expanded or collapsed.

Line 1165: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close function)

issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the `_drawer` using CSS classes, but they do not update the `aria-hidden` attribute. The drawer may remain discoverable by assistive technology even when visually hidden.

Line 1163: `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on scrollable table wrapper focus
issuesFound.explanation: The wrapper that makes the comparison table horizontally scrollable removes the outline on focus and does not provide an alternative, so keyboard users cannot see the focus ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: `.table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on brand link in footer
issuesFound.explanation: The brand link in the footer removes the outline without a replacement, breaking focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1382: `.footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the outline and do not supply a visible focus style, preventing keyboard users from tracking focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1390: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social‑link anchors
issuesFound.explanation: Social‑media anchor links remove the outline without any visible replacement, making focus invisible for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1400: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target of 44 × 44 px, making it hard to activate on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 896: `.btn-sm { min-height: 20px; /* increase to at least 44px */ min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon‑only button
issuesFound.explanation: The icon‑only button sets `min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px;` which is below the required 44 px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 923: `.btn-icon { min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px; /* increase to meet touch target */ min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which compresses spacing between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 224: `word-spacing: -0.2em; /* replace with a non‑negative value */ word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no non‑colour alternative
issuesFound.explanation: All anchor elements have their underlines removed, and the only visual cue distinguishing links from surrounding text is colour change on hover, which does not meet SC 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 252: `a { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; }`  
Line 536‑538 (add a hover style): `a:hover { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); text-decoration: underline; border-bottom-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image animates its `transform` property on hover, but there is no reduced‑motion override for this animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on feature‑card hover
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card moves upward with `transform: translateY(-2px)` on hover, but no reduced‑motion override disables this motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on pricing card hover
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing card translates upward on hover (`transform: translateY(-2px)`), with no reduced‑motion override.

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer navigation links
Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on pricing card hover

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) that opens the mobile navigation drawer never receives an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the drawer element. Without this relationship screen readers cannot announce which region will be shown/hidden.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger missing initial aria‑expanded “false”
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the mobile‑nav button does not have `aria‑expanded="false"`, so assistive technology assumes the menu is already open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers do not update aria‑expanded on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened or closed the script toggles CSS classes but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the panel’s visibility.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers missing initial aria‑expanded “false”
issuesFound.explanation: New dropdown triggers are given `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` but start without an explicit `aria-expanded="false"`, causing an ambiguous initial state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing‑period buttons do not update aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The “monthly/quarterly/yearly” buttons receive a visual “active” class but never expose their pressed state via `aria‑pressed`, so assistive tech cannot tell which period is selected.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period switch (data‑billing‑toggle) does not update aria‑checked
issuesFound.explanation: The switch element is given `role="switch"` but never receives an `aria‑checked` value, so its on/off state is invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period switch missing initial aria‑checked “false”
issuesFound.explanation: On load the switch has no `aria‑checked`, leaving its default state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix:
(see Issue 6 – the first `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', 'false');` line).  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ‑accordion triggers do not update aria‑expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded or collapsed the script animates the panel but never flips the trigger’s `aria-expanded`, so screen‑reader users cannot know the current state.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the numeric badge is changed but no ARIA live announcement is made, leaving users of assistive tech unaware of the new result count.

issuesFound.title: Pricing price changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the displayed price numbers are updated but no live‑region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users do not hear the new price.

issuesFound.title: FAQ‑accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ item changes visible content but no ARIA live announcement is made, so users of assistive technology are not informed of the state change.

fpTitles: Billing‑period switch missing initial aria‑checked “false”

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded when opened/closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `register(trigger, panel)` function in the **dropdownMenu** module adds `aria‑haspopup` and `aria‑controls` to the trigger but never sets or toggles `aria‑expanded` when the panel is opened or closed. Screen‑reader users cannot know the state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` in `_openInstance` and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` in `_closeInstance` (or use `.ariaExpanded`).

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing initial aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The same `register` routine does not initialise `aria‑expanded` on the trigger element, leaving its state undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Initialise with `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when the dropdown is registered.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter UI updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters()` updates the visible product cards, count badge and empty‑state but never writes a message to a live‑region. Users of AT are unaware when the result set changes (e.g., after applying a filter or changing the page).
issuesFound.fix:
After the animation completes, call `liveRegion.announce('Showing X products')` (or a similar dynamic message) and update an element with `role="status"`/`aria‑live="polite"`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open(item)` and `faqAccordion.close(item)` the code updates ARIA attributes and emits custom events, but it never updates a live‑region to inform screen‑reader users that a question has been expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `liveRegion.announce('Answer opened for ' + item.question)` in `open` and a corresponding “closed” announcement in `close`.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (validation functions)
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate(field)` renders error messages and adds CSS error classes, but it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` (or removes it on success). Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_renderResult`, when `result.valid` is false call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: `newsletterForm._onSubmit` calls `inlineValidator.validate(_emailEl)`, which (per Issue 5) does not set `aria-invalid`. The email input therefore lacks the required ARIA error indication.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure `inlineValidator.validate` (or the call site) sets `aria-invalid` on `_emailEl` when validation fails.

---

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: `contactForm._validateAll` uses `inlineValidator.validate(field)` for each field, but because the validator never sets `aria-invalid`, none of the contact form inputs convey error state to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Same as Issue 5 – have the validator set `aria-invalid="true"` on each invalid field and clear it on success.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce price changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod(period, animate)` updates the displayed prices on all plan cards but does not inform screen‑reader users that the billing period has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the UI, call `liveRegion.announce('Pricing switched to ' + period + ' billing')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list lacks an ARIA live region for result count updates
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateStatus` updates a status `<div>` with the number of suggestions, but this element is not given `role="status"` or `aria-live`. AT users may not hear the count of results.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_statusEl, 'role', 'status')` and `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite')` (or ensure they exist) so the count change is announced.

---

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button does not announce its activation via a live region (already does, but the live region element is hidden from AT)
issuesFound.explanation: `scrollToTop._onClick` calls `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop…)`, but the live‑region element (`#status-region`) is placed inside the DOM with `aria-live="polite"` and may be hidden from assistive tech due to CSS `display:none` when not in use. If the element is not focusable, some AT may miss the announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the live‑region element is always present (e.g., `position:absolute; width:1px; height:1px; overflow:hidden;`), not removed from the accessibility tree, so announcements are reliably delivered.

fpTitles: Newsletter form email field validation does not set aria‑invalid
Scroll‑to‑top button does not announce its activation via a live region (already does, but the live region element is hidden from AT)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle function changes the drawer’s visibility but never sets aria‑expanded on the .nav‑toggle button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') in open() and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') in close().

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav‑toggle button acts as a binary on/off control but never receives an aria‑pressed state, violating the toggle‑button pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In open() call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true'); and in close() call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav‑toggle button controls the drawer element but has no aria‑controls attribute referencing the drawer’s id, breaking the relationship for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer has an id (e.g., id="mobile-drawer") and add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'mobile-drawer') during init.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, the code only removes CSS classes and does not set aria‑hidden="true", so the drawer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open() remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick toggles the dropdown but never updates aria‑expanded on the button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and after closing, setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() adds CSS classes and animates the panel but only sets aria‑expanded to false on close; it never sets it to true on open, so assistive tech cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') at the start of open().

issuesFound.title: Product count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters() updates the numeric badge showing the number of matching products, but no live‑region is used, so users of assistive technology receive no feedback about the change.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> near the badge and update its textContent whenever the count changes.

issuesFound.title: Filter actions (category, tag, search) are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing filters updates the UI (cards, count) but does not provide an audible announcement, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: After each filter change, call liveRegion.announce(`Filter applied: ${description}`) or update a dedicated live‑region element.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new billing period
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod() updates price displays but does not inform screen‑reader users that the billing period switched (e.g., from monthly to annual).
issuesFound.fix: After changing the period, update a live‑region element or call liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing role="button"
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav‑toggle element may be a <div> or other non‑button element; without role="button” assistive technologies may not treat it as an interactive control.
issuesFound.fix: In init(), if _trigger.tagName is not BUTTON, add setAttr(_trigger, 'role', 'button').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger toggles between open and closed states but does not expose aria‑pressed, which is required for toggle‑button patterns.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true'); and in _closeInstance() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack aria‑hidden when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close() sets aria‑expanded to false on the trigger but does not set aria‑hidden="true" on the panel, so the hidden content remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In close() add setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open() remove that attribute.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation toggle button missing role="button"
Dropdown trigger button missing aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation:
Buttons that act as filter tabs indicate selection only by visual styling; they do not expose their pressed state to AT.  
  Fix (inside the map over FILTER_TABS):  
  ```tsx
  <button
    type="button"
    className={[
      'filter-tab-btn',
      activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : '',
    ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}
    tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1}
    aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}   // ← add
    onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}
  >
  ```

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation:
The monthly/annual billing toggle buttons do not convey their pressed state to AT.  
  Fix (both buttons):  
  ```tsx
  <button
    type="button"
    id="billing-monthly"
    className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}
    aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}   // ← add
    onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}
  >
  ```  
  And similarly for the annual button (`aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}`).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion button missing aria‑expanded

```tsx
    onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])}
    className="theme-toggle"
  >
    {ICONS[theme]}
  </IconButton>
  ```

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing programmatic label
issuesFound.explanation: The email <input> is wrapped by a FormGroup that renders a visual label, but the label is not associated via htmlFor or aria‑label, leaving the field unlabeled for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
add aria-label or associate the label:  
  ```tsx
  <TextInput
    id="newsletter-email"
    type="email"
    name="email"
    placeholder="you@company.com"
    autoComplete="email"
    aria-label="Email address"   // ← add
    ...
  />
  ```

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” input missing programmatic label
issuesFound.explanation: The name field’s visual label is not linked to the input, so AT cannot associate the label.
issuesFound.fix:
```tsx
  <TextInput
    id="contact-name"
    name="name"
    type="text"
    aria-label="Full name"   // ← add
    ...
  />
  ```

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” input missing programmatic label
issuesFound.explanation: Same as above for the email field.
issuesFound.fix: add aria-label="Email address" to the <TextInput>.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Subject” input missing programmatic label
issuesFound.explanation: The subject field’s label is not programmatically associated.
issuesFound.fix: add aria-label="Subject" to the <TextInput>.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” textarea missing programmatic label
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea’s visual label is not linked via htmlFor or aria‑label.
issuesFound.fix: add aria-label="Message" to the <Textarea>.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing aria‑invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails the input does not receive aria‑invalid, so AT cannot detect the error.
issuesFound.fix:
modify TextInput usage to pass aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} or set invalid prop and have TextInput apply aria-invalid. Example:  
  ```tsx
  <TextInput
    id="newsletter-email"
    ...
    aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}
  />
  ```

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” input missing aria‑invalid when in error state
issuesFound.fix: add aria-invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} to the <TextInput>.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” input missing aria‑invalid when in error state
issuesFound.fix: add aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Subject” input missing aria‑invalid when in error state
issuesFound.fix: add aria-invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error}.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” textarea missing aria‑invalid when in error state
issuesFound.fix: add aria-invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error}.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter submit button missing aria‑busy during submission
issuesFound.explanation: While the form is submitting the button does not expose its busy state, violating loading‑state communication.
issuesFound.fix:
```tsx
  <Button
    type="submit"
    variant="primary"
    size="md"
    loading={isSubmitting}          // keep
    aria-busy={isSubmitting}        // ← add
    aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
    aria-describedby={statusId}
  >
  ```

issuesFound.title: Contact form submit button missing aria‑busy during submission
issuesFound.fix: add aria-busy={isSubmitting} to the <Button> props (same pattern as Issue 22).

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation active link missing aria‑current
issuesFound.explanation:
The link marked as current via CSS class does not have aria‑current="page", so AT cannot identify the current page.  
  Fix (inside the map where `item.current` is true):  
  ```tsx
  <a
    href={item.href}
    className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}
    aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}   // ← add
  >
  ```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation active link missing aria‑current
issuesFound.fix: add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the <a> inside the mobile nav list.
