issuesFound.title: Required‑field visual marker (“*”) not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk indicating required fields is read by AT, causing noise.
issuesFound.fix: add aria-hidden="true" to the . ```tsx * ```

issuesFound.title: Duplicate integration list (visual clone) not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The cloned list is purely decorative for the marquee but is exposed to AT, leading to duplicate content.
issuesFound.fix:
add aria-hidden="true" to the cloned <ul>.  
  ```tsx
  <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">
  ```

issuesFound.title: Hidden testimonial slides not marked with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Slides that are not currently visible are still exposed to AT, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix:
add aria-hidden={!isVisible} to the slide container.  
  ```tsx
  <div
    key={t.id}
    role="group"
    aria-roledescription="slide"
    aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`}
    aria-hidden={!isVisible}   // ← add
    className={[
      'testimonial-slide',
      isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : '',
    ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}
  >
  ```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop missing aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop is decorative but is not hidden from AT, causing it to be announced.
issuesFound.fix:
add aria-hidden="true" to the backdrop <div>.  
  ```tsx
  <div
    className="mobile-nav-backdrop"
    aria-hidden="true"
    onClick={onClose}
  />
  ```

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions within the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1382: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 1404: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initial state does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init()` function initializes the navigation controller but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute on the trigger button if the menu is closed by default. This leaves the initial state ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1446: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav dynamically hidden elements not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions set the `hidden` attribute on `_drawer` and `_overlay` but do not synchronize the `aria-hidden` attribute. When hidden, these elements should have `aria-hidden="true"` to remove them from the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` functions within `dropdownMenu` manage the panel visibility but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the associated trigger element (`inst.trigger`).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1533: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1551: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu initial state does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu.register()` function sets up the dropdown instance but does not initialize the trigger's `aria-expanded` attribute to "false" when the panel is hidden by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1606: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu dynamically hidden panel not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` functions toggle the `hidden` attribute on `inst.panel` but do not update `aria-hidden`. The panel should be removed from the accessibility tree when hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1530: inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 1554: inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_open()` and `_close()` functions in `siteSearch` manage the suggestion listbox visibility but do not update `aria-expanded` on the search input (`_inputEl`). This violates the combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1998: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 2009: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs do not update aria-pressed or aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates()` function updates the visual active class on filter tabs but does not update `aria-pressed` (for buttons) or `aria-checked` (for radios) to reflect the selected state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2283: setAttr(btn, isActive ? ARIA.PRESSED : 'aria-checked', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters()` function updates the product count badge (`_countBadge`) when filters change, but does not announce this change to screen readers via a live region. Users are unaware the results have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2237: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button visibility not synchronized with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `_update()` function in `scrollToTop` toggles the `hidden` attribute on the button based on scroll position but does not update `aria-hidden`. The button remains in the accessibility tree even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1735: _btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 1743: _btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing module dynamic update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod()` function updates all price displays on the page when the billing period changes, but does not announce this significant content change to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2506: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid()` function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field itself. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid without this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1235: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid()` function removes the error description but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"` on the field. The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1248: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle functions (`open`, `close`, `toggle`) change the visibility of the drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users are not informed when the menu state changes. Additionally, `aria-expanded` is not initialized to "false" on load.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the FAQ accordion module removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where screen readers may not announce the item as expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function: faqAccordion.open: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not manage aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox trigger but `aria-expanded` is never set on the input element (`_inputEl`) when the suggestion list opens or closes. The `mount` function also fails to initialize this attribute. Screen reader users are not notified when the suggestion list becomes visible.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle is assigned `role="switch"` in `init`, but the `_syncToggleState` function only toggles CSS classes. It does not update the `aria-checked` attribute to reflect the current state (monthly vs annual). Screen reader users perceive the switch state incorrectly.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function: pricing._syncToggleState: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` utility function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Form validation errors are not programmatically exposed to assistive technologies as invalid inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function: srUtils.markInvalid: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` utility function removes the error description but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"` on the field. Inputs that have been corrected remain marked as invalid in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function: srUtils.markValid: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions manage the `hidden` attribute and CSS classes on the drawer (`_drawer`) but do not manage `aria-hidden`. When closed, the navigation content remains in the accessibility tree, causing clutter for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Function: mobileNav.open: _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  Line Function: mobileNav.close: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel visibility is toggled via the `hidden` attribute, but `aria-hidden` is not synchronized. Closed dropdown panels remain accessible to assistive technology, creating navigation noise.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Function: dropdownMenu._openInstance: inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  Line Function: dropdownMenu._closeInstance: inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion `open` and `close` functions toggle the `hidden` attribute on the answer panel but do not update `aria-hidden`. Collapsed answers remain in the accessibility tree, confusing screen reader navigation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Function: faqAccordion.open: item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  Line Function: faqAccordion.close: item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button visibility is managed by the `_update` function using the `hidden` attribute and CSS classes, but `aria-hidden` is not toggled. When hidden (at top of page), the button is still focusable/announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Function: scrollToTop._update (shouldShow block): _btn.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  Line Function: scrollToTop._update (!shouldShow block): _btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions emit custom DOM events but do not announce the state change (e.g., "Navigation menu opened") to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the mobile menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Function: mobileNav.open: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
  Line Function: mobileNav.close: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open/close functions emit events but do not write to the live region. Users relying on screen readers are not notified when a dropdown menu expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Function: dropdownMenu._openInstance: liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');
  Line Function: dropdownMenu._closeInstance: liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function updates prices and emits an event but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to the live region. Screen reader users miss the context of the price update.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function: pricing.setPeriod: liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: productFilter action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters` function updates the product grid and count badge but does not announce the result (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to the live region. Screen reader users are unaware that the content has changed after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function: productFilter.applyFilters: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) || 'Products filtered');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open function does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce count change

issuesFound.title: pricing _updatePlanCard does not announce price change

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator _animate does not announce number change

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader _onScroll does not update aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons include transform in their transition property and apply a translateY transform on :active, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 782: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on hero primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button applies a translateY transform on hover without a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the movement for users who request reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 782: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image applies perspective transforms and transitions on both the base state and hover state without any override in the empty prefers-reduced-motion media query. Users with motion sensitivity may experience discomfort from the 3D rotation effects.
issuesFound.fix: Line 782: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card applies a translateY and scale transform on hover that is not covered by the existing prefers-reduced-motion media query (which only targets the base .pricing-card:hover). Users requesting reduced motion will still see the movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1162: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities. Negative word-spacing makes text harder to parse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 166: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on active filter tab button
issuesFound.explanation: The active filter tab uses var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) text on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.65:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text at 14px/500 weight.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1006: color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); (where --colour-brand-primary-hover: #004aaa provides 7.5:1 contrast) or alternatively Line 1005: background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); Line 1006: color: var(--colour-text-on-brand);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on active pricing toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The active pricing toggle button uses white text (#ffffff) on var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.65:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1130: background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); (where --colour-brand-primary-hover: #004aaa provides 7.5:1 contrast with white)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor selector removes text underlines from all links without providing a non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom, font-weight change, or background highlight for the default state. This makes links indistinguishable from surrounding text for colour-blind users unless they hover over them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 236: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add Line 236: a { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or a non-zero border. This removes the default focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide content. It lacks the necessary clip or clip-path properties and does not use absolute positioning, meaning screen-reader-only text remains visible to sighted users and breaks the layout.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350-361: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.explanation: The pricing section header paragraph uses light gray text (#aaaaaa) on a subtle off-white background (#f8fafc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1. This fails WCAG AA standards.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .contact-section > p foreground #aaaaaa / background #f8fafc ratio 2.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: The contact section introductory paragraph uses #aaaaaa text on #f8fafc background, with a contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1. This fails the required 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .logo-bar-label foreground #94a3b8 / background #f8fafc ratio 2.6:1

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer navigation links foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 ratio 1.4:1

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .social-links a foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 ratio 1.4:1
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links use #94a3b8 text on the dark footer background (#555555), with a contrast ratio of approximately 1.4:1. This fails WCAG standards and makes these links unreadable.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-brand address foreground #64748b / background #555555 ratio 1.2:1

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-bottom > p foreground #64748b / background #555555 ratio 1.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom text uses #64748b on #555555 background, with a contrast ratio of approximately 1.2:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for text contrast.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1432: .footer-bottom > p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); margin: 0; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links inherit text-decoration: none from the global a selector and have no alternative visual differentiator such as font-weight increase or border-bottom. They rely solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: [id$="-submenu"] li a { display: block; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); color: var(--colour-text-primary); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; min-height: 28px; display: flex; align-items: center; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove underlines globally and do not provide an alternative non-colour visual differentiator in their default state. They rely on colour alone (#94a3b8) to distinguish them from text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1416: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links in the footer remove underlines and rely solely on colour to indicate they are links. Without underlines or other visual cues, users with colour vision deficiencies may not recognize these as interactive links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1427: .social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 28px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative: .footer-brand address a
issuesFound.explanation: Links within the footer address block remove underlines and lack alternative visual differentiators. These links rely on colour alone, which is insufficient for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1403: .footer-brand address a { color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); display: inline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator in high-contrast mode without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset and border-radius, lacking a visible focus ring.

issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links set outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator without replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links set outline: none with only outline-offset, lacking a visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Button components set outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the default focus ring without a visible replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button base class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended target size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: Hero CTA primary button sets min-height: 32px, below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .hero-ctas .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: Hero CTA secondary button sets min-height: 32px, below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: Mobile navigation toggle sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, below the 24px absolute minimum and well below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #nav-menu > li > a and button
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation menu items set min-height: 28px, below the 24px absolute minimum and recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set min-height: 28px, below minimum accessibility thresholds.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: Search input sets height: 28px, below the 24px minimum and 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: Search submit button sets width: 28px and height: 28px, below accessibility minimums.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: Small button variant sets min-height: 20px, below the 24px WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-lg
issuesFound.explanation: Large button variant sets min-height: 36px, below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only button sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px absolute minimum target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons set min-height: 28px, below minimum accessibility thresholds.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons set min-height: 28px, below the 24px minimum and 44px recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .product-body .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: Product card secondary button sets min-height: 28px, below accessibility minimums.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs set width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px, meeting the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended and 24px minimum target size.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible instead of the correct visually-hidden technique (position: absolute, 1px dimensions, clip). This makes the content visible to sighted users and potentially hides it from screen readers inconsistently, breaking accessibility for skip links and screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: Skip link uses transition: transform which causes spatial movement, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 427 is empty and does not disable this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: Hero CTA button includes transform in its transition property, but no reduced-motion override exists to disable this spatial animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: Hero image uses transition: transform for spatial movement on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 574 is empty.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: Hero image hover state applies transform: perspective(...) rotateY(...) without a reduced-motion override to disable the spatial animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: Featured pricing card hover uses transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) for spatial movement, but the reduced-motion media query at line 766 only covers the base .pricing-card:hover, not the featured variant.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Button base class includes transform in its transition property, with no reduced-motion override to disable spatial animations.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: Button active state uses transform: translateY(1px) for spatial movement without a reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: Body text sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces spacing between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: Section header paragraphs use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on --colour-bg-page (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for large text (3:1) and normal text (4.5:1).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card paragraphs use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on #ffffff background, providing only ~2.1:1 contrast ratio, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal 14px text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .step-content p
issuesFound.explanation: Step content paragraphs use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background, resulting in ~2.1:1 contrast ratio, below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .product-body > p:not(.product-tagline)
issuesFound.explanation: Product card descriptions use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background, providing insufficient contrast at ~2.1:1 for 14px text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .pricing-section .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing section header paragraphs use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background. At 18px font-size (large text), this requires 3:1 contrast but only provides ~2.1:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .plan-tagline
issuesFound.explanation: Plan taglines use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background, resulting in ~2.1:1 contrast for 14px text, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .pricing-toggle > span
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle labels use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background, providing ~2.1:1 contrast for 14px text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: Input hints use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background. At 12px font-size, this provides ~2.1:1 contrast, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .newsletter-section > p
issuesFound.explanation: Newsletter paragraph uses rgba(255,255,255,0.8) on a gradient background containing #bb88ee (light purple). Against the light purple portion, white text provides only ~1.4:1 contrast, and the 80% opacity reduces this further, failing contrast requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-brand address
issuesFound.explanation: Footer address uses --colour-neutral-500 (#64748b) on --colour-neutral-900 (#1e293b) background, resulting in approximately 3.9:1 contrast ratio, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom text uses --colour-neutral-500 (#64748b) on #1e293b background, providing ~3.9:1 contrast, below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal 12px text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without additional non-colour visual differentiators (such as border-bottom or font-weight) in the normal state, links are only distinguishable by colour, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not set aria-expanded to reflect the open/closed state of their associated panels. Assistive technology users cannot perceive the visibility state of the dropdown content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2283: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 2293: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not update aria-expanded when the suggestion listbox is shown or hidden. Screen reader users are not notified of the popup state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3125: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line 3148: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Category filter tab buttons do not set aria-pressed to reflect the active filter state. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3526: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: pricing price changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When switching billing periods, the price values update visually but are not announced to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology miss the price change feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3802: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices updated to ' + period + ' billing');` after updating all plan cards.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ answers does not trigger a live region announcement. Users may not realize the content has expanded or collapsed, especially since focus remains on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4132: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question));` after setting aria-expanded.
  Line 4162: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));` after setting aria-expanded to false.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users may be unaware that navigation options are now available or have been hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2162: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` after `_lockScroll();`
  Line 2180: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` after `_unlockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu open/close actions lack live region announcements. Users may not perceive that a submenu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2287: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` after emitting dropdown:open.
  Line 2297: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` after emitting dropdown:close.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid on errors
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the code adds error classes and descriptions but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4365: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the `else` block (result.valid === false).

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the function removes error styling and describedby references but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field may still be reported as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1068: Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after clearing describedby.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button lacks aria-controls pointing to the drawer element's ID. Users cannot identify which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2208: Add `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav');` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId);` after getting _drawer.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are not initialized with aria-expanded="false". The initial state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2342: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer closes, it is visually hidden via CSS class but remains in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users may still navigate to hidden content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2174: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
  Line 2158: Add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` or `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` in open() after adding the open class.

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count updates and cards show/hide, but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed of the results of their filter action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3505: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) + '. ' + totalMatches + ' products found');` inside the filterAnimation.run callback.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus not moved to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved to the drawer content or trapped within it. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button and cannot access the navigation links without manually tabbing.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus not returned to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer closes, focus is not returned to the toggle button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2172: Add `const triggerToFocus = _trigger;` at the start of the function, then after `_unlockScroll();` add `if (triggerToFocus) triggerToFocus.focus();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu focus not returned to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown closes (e.g., via Escape key), focus is not returned to the trigger button. The returnFocus parameter exists but is not implemented.

issuesFound.title: toastManager auto-dismiss cannot be paused
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications auto-dismiss after 4-6 seconds without a mechanism to pause the timer. Users with reading disabilities may not have enough time to read the message before it disappears.

fpTitles: mobileNav focus not moved to drawer
mobileNav focus not returned to trigger
toastManager auto-dismiss cannot be paused

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels are shown and hidden, but the trigger button's aria-expanded state is never synchronized. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [in _openInstance, after `inst.open = true;`]: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line [in _closeInstance, after `inst.open = false;`]: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but its aria-expanded attribute is never updated when the suggestion listbox opens or closes. Screen readers cannot convey whether suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [in _open function]: `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line [in _close function]: `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) visually indicate active state via CSS classes but do not expose their pressed state to assistive technologies via aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line [inside _periodBtns.forEach, after toggleClass]: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement for billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the visual prices but does not announce the change or the new prices to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line [after _updateProgress(_current) in _goToStep, or after setPeriod logic]: `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to \${period} billing\`);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, it links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers may not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line [in markInvalid, after addDescribedBy]: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the code removes the error description but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute, leaving stale invalid state on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line [in markValid, after removing describedby]: `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load, so screen readers may not recognize it as a disclosure control.
issuesFound.fix: Line [in init, after _initialised = true;]: `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line [in init, after registering instances]: `_instances.forEach(inst => setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false));`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line [in init, after _initialised = true;]: `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is visually hidden but not removed from the accessibility tree with aria-hidden="true", so screen reader users may still navigate to it.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [in close function, after removeClass]: `if (_drawer) setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`
  Line [in open function, after addClass]: `if (_drawer) removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are hidden with the hidden attribute but aria-hidden is not synchronized, which can cause inconsistent announcements in some browser/AT combinations.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [in _closeInstance, after setTimeout]: `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`
  Line [in _openInstance, after removeAttribute]: `removeAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestion list missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox is shown/hidden using the hidden attribute, but aria-hidden is not toggled to ensure it is properly excluded from the accessibility tree when closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [in hide function]: `if (_listEl) setAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`
  Line [in show function]: `if (_listEl) removeAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap. When the drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the navigation, allowing users to tab out of the modal drawer into the background content.

issuesFound.title: navKeyboardShortcuts using Alt+key modifiers
issuesFound.explanation: The keyboard shortcuts (Alt+S, Alt+N, Alt+M, Alt+F, Alt+T) may conflict with screen reader shortcuts (e.g., NVDA, JAWS, or VoiceOver use Alt combinations for their own navigation), preventing users from accessing site features.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [in _onKeydown, before processing]: 
  ```
  if (e.altKey && (e.shiftKey || e.ctrlKey)) return;
  ```
  Or change shortcuts to use Ctrl+Shift+key or provide a way to disable them.

issuesFound.title: contactForm missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields (full_name, email, subject, message) are not programmatically identified with autocomplete attributes, preventing browsers and assistive technologies from automatically filling them.
issuesFound.fix:
Line [after binding inlineValidator for each field, or in field configuration]:
  For full_name field: `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`
  For email field: `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`
  For subject field: `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'off');` or appropriate value
  For message field: `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'off');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter count updates not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ disclosure buttons do not have aria-expanded attributes to indicate whether the answer panel is visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-controls={panelId} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only close button in the mobile navigation panel lacks an aria-label, making it inaccessible to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button contains only an emoji icon and lacks an aria-label to describe its purpose to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button contains only a character entity and lacks an aria-label to describe its function.
issuesFound.fix: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button contains only a character entity and lacks an aria-label to describe its function.
issuesFound.fix: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references an error message ID via aria-describedby when in error state, but no element with that ID exists because FormGroup is not configured to render the error message with the matching ID.
issuesFound.fix: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'contact-name-error' via aria-describedby but the error message element is not rendered with this ID because FormGroup lacks the errorId prop.
issuesFound.fix: <TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" autoComplete="name" required aria-required="true" value={name.state.value} onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={name.onBlur} invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'contact-email-error' via aria-describedby but the error message element is not rendered with this ID.
issuesFound.fix: <TextInput id="contact-email" name="email" type="email" autoComplete="email" required aria-required="true" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'contact-subject-error' via aria-describedby but the error message element is not rendered with this ID.
issuesFound.fix: <TextInput id="contact-subject" name="subject" type="text" required aria-required="true" value={subject.state.value} onChange={(ev) => subject.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={subject.onBlur} invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea references 'contact-message-error' via aria-describedby but the error message element is not rendered with this ID.
issuesFound.fix: <Textarea id="contact-message" name="message" rows={6} required aria-required="true" value={message.state.value} onChange={(ev) => message.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={message.onBlur} invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-describedby={counterId} />

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker for required fields is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce "star" separately from the "(required)" text that follows.
issuesFound.fix: *

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link uses a visual class to indicate the current page but lacks aria-current="page" to communicate this state programmatically to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link in the mobile menu uses a visual class but lacks aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: <a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element containing skip links lacks an aria-label, making it difficult for screen reader users to distinguish from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The main navigation element lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation regions on the page.
issuesFound.fix: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav panel navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element inside the mobile panel lacks an aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: <nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby to identify it as a distinct region.
issuesFound.fix: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby to identify the region.
issuesFound.fix: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card lacks aria-labelledby to associate it with its heading, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the card's purpose when navigating by landmark.
issuesFound.fix: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard CTA link missing contextual accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The call-to-action link uses generic text without including the plan name, making it indistinguishable from other plan CTAs when read out of context.
issuesFound.fix: <a href={plan.ctaHref} aria-label={`${plan.ctaLabel} for ${plan.name} plan`} className={['btn', plan.featured ? 'btn--primary' : 'btn--outline', 'btn--md'].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar visual clone not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for the marquee visual effect lacks aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the integration partners twice.
issuesFound.fix: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state, it does not communicate this status to assistive technology via aria-busy.
issuesFound.fix: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons do not communicate their selected state via aria-pressed, relying solely on visual styling.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>
  Fix: Line 2: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons do not communicate their active/pressed state programmatically, relying only on visual styling and tabindex.
issuesFound.fix: <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The visual chevron indicator is not marked as aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the arrow character in addition to the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: Button spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner visual element is not marked with aria-hidden, potentially causing redundant announcements alongside the loading label.
issuesFound.fix: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

fpTitles: ContactForm subject input broken aria-describedby reference
